title: Break retain cycles with weak/unowned
description: Capture self weakly in escaping closures and delegate references so view controllers and objects deallocate instead of leaking.
body: Use when a closure, timer, delegate, or Combine subscription captures self. Do not strongly capture self in an escaping closure stored by the same object, and do not use strong delegate references. Actions: use [weak self] (then guard) in escaping closures, unowned only when self is guaranteed to outlive the closure, weak delegates, and store/cancel Combine subscriptions so they release. Validation signals include Instruments/leak checks showing view controllers deallocate on dismiss and no growing object counts. Risk boundary: a retain cycle leaks the whole view controller and its subtree, growing memory until the app is jettisoned — under ARC there is no GC to clean it up, so the cycle must be broken explicitly.
